hypothesis: Augmenting a uMLIP pretrained on OMat24 with 5,000 DFT-calculated out-of-equilibrium snapshots specifically sampling diverse MOF metal–ligand coordination environments (covering ≥10 metal species and ≥50 linker chemistries) will reduce the mean absolute error in predicted bulk modulus on a held-out MOF test set by at least 25% and lower the fraction of MD runs exhibiting volume deviations >10% by at least 30% compared to the base uMLIP.
